reviews.author: Zorro Lang
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Zorro Lang jokingly expressed concern that the patch title '[PATCH 0/2] squota delete fstests' might be a critical bug report about fstests deleting itself, but it's actually just a test case for squota qgroup deletion.

reviewer questioned the relevance of this patch to the overall submission and suggested reviewing it separately from patch 2/2
reviews.raw_body:
Hahah, to be honest, the title '[PATCH 0/2] squota delete fstests' gave me
a minor heart attack. I thought it was a critical bug report about fstests
accidentally deleting itself while running :-D

---

This change doesn't seem related to patch 2/2. I think we can review
these 2 patches separately.

For this patch, I'm fine with or without it. If the btrfs list prefers
to have it, or move it to be a common helper, I'm good with that.

Thanks,
Zorro

2026-05-14.patch_summary: This patch resurrects a previous attempt to add test cases for edge scenarios involving the deletion of quota groups in Btrfs file systems, incorporating recent bug fixes and feedback.

2026-05-13.patch_summary: This patch series resurrects a previous attempt to land tests for squota qgroup deletion edge cases, incorporating additional testing and feedback.

reviews.author: Boris Burkov (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that Filipe's feedback was correct and agreed to split the patches into separate submissions, no fix planned but will follow original intent
reviews.raw_body:
I pulled this into a separate patch based on Filipe's feedback on the
old version I failed to get in. Agreed it doesn't need to be a series,
just felt related enough to send in a batch, due to the context.

Thanks for taking a look,
Boris

2026-05-15.patch_summary: This patch resurrects a previous attempt to add test cases for edge scenarios involving the deletion of quota groups in Btrfs file systems, incorporating recent bug fixes and feedback.

reviews.author: Zorro Lang
reviews.summary: reviewer suggested merging one patch independently of the other as long as one has an Ack, noting that the patches are not dependent on each other
reviews.raw_body:
Don't worry. What I mean is that as long as one of these two patches gets an
Ack first, even if the other is still being questioned, we can merge it first.
After all, they are independent of each other.

2026-05-16.patch_summary: This patch resurrects a previous attempt to add test cases for edge scenarios involving the deletion of quota groups in Btrfs file systems, incorporating recent bug fixes and feedback.
